title: aiorch Pipeline v1
description: Schema for aiorch YAML pipeline definitions. Validates structure and field types. Semantic validation (DAG cycles, dependency references, template variables) is handled by the Python runtime.

name.description: Pipeline name. Used as identifier across the system.

env.description: Environment variables injected into all steps.

input.description: Pipeline input parameters. Values can be simple defaults or rich typed definitions.

outputs.description: Pipeline output declarations. 'result' names the primary output variable, 'artifacts' lists saved file outputs.

steps.description: Pipeline steps. Each key is the step name, each value is a step definition with exactly one primitive (run, prompt, agent, action, flow).

inputField.description: Rich input field definition with type, default, and metadata.

type.description: Input type. Retired types (file, json, csv, env) are rejected — use 'artifact' with the appropriate 'format' for files, or workspace_secrets / workspace_configs for environment values. Connector inputs are Platform-only.

default.description: Default value if not provided at runtime.

minimum.description: Inclusive lower bound for integer / number inputs. Values below this are rejected by validate_inputs() at run start.

maximum.description: Inclusive upper bound for integer / number inputs. Values above this are rejected by validate_inputs() at run start.

format.description: How to interpret artifact bytes. Also used by http type for response parsing (text|json).

content_type.description: MIME type hint for artifact inputs (e.g., 'text/plain', 'application/json'). Used for upload validation.

max_size_mb.description: Per-input size cap override. Falls back to workspace default.

delimiter.description: CSV delimiter for artifact inputs with format: csv.

method.description: HTTP method for http input type.

uses.description: Connector name for type: connector inputs and action: connector steps. Resolved at run time via the scope chain (pipeline -> workspace -> org).

operation.description: Canonical operation name for connector inputs (query, read, list, etc.).

sql.description: SQL statement for connector database operations. Parameterized — values go in 'params', NOT in the string.

max_rows.description: Row cap for connector database query results.

key.description: Object store key for connector read/write/delete operations.

prefix.description: Object store prefix for connector list operations.

limit.description: Result cap for connector list operations.

step.description: A single pipeline step. Must contain exactly one primitive field: run, prompt, agent, action, or flow.

run.description: Shell command to execute. Supports Jinja2 templates.

prompt.description: LLM prompt text. Supports Jinja2 templates.

agent.description: Agent definition — string (system prompt) or object with system, tools, mcp.

action.description: Action type: slack, webhook, github-comment, github-issue, write-file, email, s3, kafka, teams, discord.

python.description: Inline Python source. Runs in the executor's own interpreter (not a subprocess). The body has access to `inputs` (dict of all in-scope context vars) and should assign a JSON-serializable value to `result` to emit a step output. Captured stdout falls through as the output when `result` is not set.

input.description: Step input — file path, variable reference, or list of inputs.

vars.description: Step-local variables merged into template context.

model.description: LLM model override for this step (e.g. 'gpt-4o', 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514').

mcp.description: MCP server configuration for agent tool access.

config.description: Action-specific parameters. Each action type defines its own config shape. Examples: {channel, message} for slack, {url, method, headers, body} for webhook, {path, body} for write-file, {to, subject, body} for email, {topic, value, key} for kafka, {operation, url, body} for s3.

depends.description: Step names this step depends on. All must complete before this step runs.

parallel.description: Run in parallel with another step (string name) or boolean for foreach.

condition.description: Jinja2 expression. Step is skipped if this evaluates to false.

retry.description: Number of retry attempts on failure.

cache.description: Cache LLM responses for identical prompts.

foreach.description: Iterate over a list. Each iteration gets 'item' in context.

output.description: Variable name to store this step's result. Available to downstream steps as {{name}}.

format.description: Output format. 'json' parses LLM response as JSON.

save.description: File path to save output to.

schema.description: JSON Schema to validate prompt output against. Implies format: json.

retry_on_invalid.description: Re-prompt LLM with validation errors this many times.

assertions.description: Python-like assertion expressions evaluated against output.

max_cost.description: Maximum cost in USD for this step.

on_failure.description: Step name to run if this step fails.

trigger.description: When to run: 'on_failure' means only run as error handler.
